statement: The system shall provide predefined movements to assist users in regaining muscle memory.
The system shall provide a baseline configuration and support variant configurations with extended arm length, increased lifting capacity, and waterproofing as required by projects.
The system shall enter a safe mode when a movement is not programmed or when movement speed exceeds safe limits.
The system shall enforce hard stops in software and ensure hardware also enforces hard stops.
The system shall display the joint range for each joint on the MMI screens and change the display colour when limits are approached.
The system shall allocate a time allowance for tasks equal to the estimated time plus 50 percent.
The system shall provide cable stops or guides to keep the cable within defined boundaries.
The system shall model the entire scene exactly in simulation.
The system shall support teach files that enable programming point-to-point movements without joystick control.
The system shall allow programming repeatable tasks such as tool change positions and provide a home button that returns the arm to a known state.
The system shall move autonomously close to the target and then hand over control to the operator.
The system shall provide haptic feedback to the operator when motion is incorrect.
The system shall provide a variable camera viewpoint in the UI to improve depth perception.
The system shall provide fixed camera views that remain constant for specific tasks.
The system shall allow operators to adjust the zoom level in the UI, supporting both zoomed-out and zoomed-in views.
The system shall provide an augmented‑reality overlay that guides the operator when performing new tasks.
The system shall provide the visual feed at the highest possible image resolution at all times.
The system shall prioritize resolution over frame rate.
The system shall provide force feedback to indicate when the operator is moving in the correct direction and cease the feedback when the operator is not moving in the correct direction.
The system shall include a ghost mode that displays a predicted end position before execution.
The system shall display the planned trajectory and end position for pre‑planned movements in ghost mode.
The system shall define no‑go zones and prevent movement into those areas.
The system shall sound an audible alarm when the operator drifts toward a no‑go zone.
The system shall recognise different materials such as rubber, aluminium, and painted surfaces using computer vision.
The system shall use material recognition results to create no‑go zones for prohibited materials.
The system shall detect overload conditions and release the grip to prevent dropping objects.
